experiences.title: Senior Principal Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription: part of the postgres database team which works on encryption and decryption of individual columns with different keys.

experiences.title: Senior Software Engineer (Remote)
experiences.jobDescription:
worked on POC to enable columnar storage
for Postgres tables by decoding WAL logs, using Flatbuf moved data from C to kotlin structs and converting transactions to parquet files in
Apache Delta lake using Kotlin. Worked on few bug fixes in Apache Datafusion using Rust. I had
designed the solution for Archival of Delta Tables to Azure Blob storage.

experiences.title: Senior Principal Software Engineer (Remote)
experiences.jobDescription:
I was working on implementation of Encryption of all
database columns whose keys are never known to database engines such as MariaDB,
Postgres and MySQL. 
Designed and Implemented the Intel SGX Enclave library to handle
encryption/decryption, comparison, wildcard comparison, arithmetic, aggregation and
ordering of encrypted columns.

experiences.title: Expert, System Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
I have driven the second part of SQL/MX Executor 64-bit project, provided design solutions for multiple complex issues and implemented quick solutions without impacting various sub components of NonStop Server.
Currently working on design and implementation of Analytic functions and Time Series clause.

experiences.title: Senior Specialist, System Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Part of SQL/MX database Executor team working in new feature development, bug fixes and initiatives using C++.
•	Designed and implemented SELECT FOR UPDATE feature and BINARY and VARBINARY datatypes in SQL/MX R3.6 release
•	Won “Quality Award” on 2017 at Business Unit level for driving the initiative “Java Framework for SQL/MX regressions”
•	Won “Rock Star” award on 2016 at Business Unit level for “ComposIT” initiative using Node.js, AngularJS and HPOO.

experiences.title: Project Leader - Web Technologies
experiences.jobDescription: I was part of web technologies team and worked in product development using Angular js, Node js and Node webkit. Also worked in a system integration project to build and run the daemon in Android mobile and communicate with desktop application using TCP sockets.

experiences.title: Freelancer and Software Architect
experiences.jobDescription:
Developing Mobile applications for all mobile platforms including IOS, Android, Windows phone 7 & 8 and amazon fire OS. 
Developed hybrid app 'WordToonZ' using phonegap.
Used HTML5, CSS3, Javascript for responsive and animated UI.

experiences.title: Principal Member Technical Staff
experiences.jobDescription:
I was part of MySQL Enterprise Backup team.
Projects worked:

MySQL Enterprise Backup 3.8 – Parallelizing all its operations

            This project is the initiative proposed to redesign the existing backup and recovery product MEB 3.7 for parallelizing its sub operations so that we gain the performance improvement. Read, process and write sub-operations of all MEB operations could run in parallel. These sub-operations could also be sub-divided within themselves and run in parallel. MEB 3.8 supports “Block-level” parallelism -- ie., each thread handles a portion in a single file. That is, different segments of a single file could be read/process/write in parallel. This makes the MEB to perform better for both system tablespaces with huge single ibdata files as well as tablespaces with lots of smaller per table IBD files. This effort provides the performance improvement of 200% to 1000% comparing to MEB 3.7. 

Single Step Restore using MySQL Enterprise Backup

•	Designed and worked as a three member team to enable single step restore of directory and image (uncompressed or compressed or incremental) backups by removing all intermediate steps and temporary disk space requirement.

experiences.title: SSE
experiences.jobDescription:
I was part of GOPS team, PayPal India and worked on features and bug fixes in Admin and Attack products.

I am part of GOPS team which is owner for Admin and Attack products. The Admin product is responsible for providing interface to “customer support agents” of PayPal to access the customer account details and manage them. Attack is responsible for managing the customer reported cases and automating the business rules with less user interaction. I have worked on 2 major projects and 1 feature. I have worked on more than 5 major production issues and resolved them.

experiences.title: SE
experiences.jobDescription:
	Worked in the various critical customer production issues and helped them out to workaround those issues in quick turn-around and fixed those issues.
	Mentored 3 new team members and help them to be more productive with in 3 months.
	Played the role of “Quality controller” for the team, ensured the quality in the code-deliveries and helped the team to understand importance of process.
	Played the role of “RVU coordinator”, planned the RVU schedule for the product release and coordinated with CM and QA team.

mxexportddl/mximportddl utility - redesign

     This project is the agile initiative proposed to redesign the existing utilities for stabilizing these internal utilities and release them to customers and involves more than 16 KLOC changes. These utilities could replicate the table's metadata and statistics from one system to other system in robust and efficient way. These utilities do not replicate the data. The usability and flexibility of these utilities were enriched by providing various command options and readable outputs.

experiences.title: SE
experiences.jobDescription: Developed "Exception handling mechanism"  for table oriented programming language DELUGE.

recommendationsReceived.name: Prapulla S Bangalore
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: manager at hp
recommendationsReceived.description: Dhamotharan is a brilliant engineer, very curious about how everything works and constantly looking out for innovations in the product. He worked with SQL/MX and added useful innovative features for the same. His debugging skills are very good and can turn around an emergency situation with customer with quick workarounds and temporary fixes. He is an intense learner and can take to new technology in a very short time.

recommendationsReceived.name: Ashok Viswanathan
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Director of Software Engineering at Rocket Software
recommendationsReceived.description: I found Dhamotharan a "go-to" person - he could take up a technical problem and solve it in little time. I used to turn to him  when we had to meet our customer needs under great pressure. He will stretch, spend sleepless nights,  and you will start getting concerned for him, but he will keep persisting till he gets the breakthrough. You now know what I mean by a "go-to" person.  He is what I would call a "great" engineer as he could solve issues using unconventional thinking; he also has a fantastic capability to churn out code at a good rate. He made a big difference with his databases knowledge. I admire the courage he used to show in taking up a challenge - and it is courage which makes the difference, as one can go wrong often. Yet his self-belief made the difference in whatever he took up - he would make the hardest of technical issues, look very surmountable and make a difference. This attitude alone made him knowledgeable  in many areas of the code which he worked on even though he was not familiar with them - as he just learned it by plunging into it. Dhamotharan gained a good number of admirers, who wanted to learn from him and he became the inadvertent mentor to others in the team who were new. A good leader as he could take care of not only his deliverables but also guide his peers in doing theirs. It is no surprise to me that he is now looking for more challenges, he will change the status-quo in any  team he becomes part of. Dhamotharan has left a big impression on me, and I am glad that I have had the fortune of working with him. I wish him well, and success will come by him.

recommendationsReceived.name: Hema Sridharan
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Manager – Release Management | Senior Consultant | Enterprise Delivery | DevOps Automation & Release Governance
recommendationsReceived.context: Hema worked with Dhamotharan on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description:
It was pleasure to work with Dhamo in the backup team. He is a very skilled, dedicated developer and very capable software engineer who goes indepth to understand the technical aspects of the product. At the same time he also makes sure to maintain the quality of the code. He would always come up with new ideas with the motive of user intuitiveness of the product. When there was almost no new members in the backup team, Dhamo was the first to join and he uplifted the MEB 3.8 release with major contribution. He implemented the parallel backup feature which involves lot of refactoring of the code. His profundity in the subject gives an ability to excel not only as developer but also as software/solution architect.

He is a very good mentor from time to time to QA and other dev folks in the team to explain the code changes for the new features.

I would recommend Dhamo for any kind of software related projects.

recommendationsReceived.name: Shreekala Kurup
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Co-Founder and Chief Mentor at Crackverbal, MBA and Masters Admission Consultant
recommendationsReceived.context: Shreekala was senior to Dhamotharan but didn't manage Dhamotharan directly
recommendationsReceived.description: Dhamotharan is one of the most technically sharp people we had in our team at HP. He always had this attitude that any problem has a solution. And he made sure that he did his best on whatever he did.I am sure he will do wonders with his training and freelancing plan.

recommendationsReceived.name: Jothir Ganesan
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Manager, Analytics Customer Excellence at Oracle
recommendationsReceived.context: Jothir worked with Dhamotharan on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: Dhamotharan is a sincere and hardworking person. He is dedicated at work and completes tasks assigned to him on time.. He is brilliant in coming up with working prototypes for complicated problems and implementing those efficiently. For eg., Implement parallelism while taking a backup of a database with multiple threads. He has taken the sole responsibility of rewriting the entire code in C++ for better usability. He is good in assisting his juniors to understand and develop code. He is passionate about what he does and is highly confident in getting the task accomplished. He is highly credible for solving customer issues of high severity given at any point of time. Overall I would recommend Dhamo as a strong individual, committed to his work and  very friendly to team members. A great person to work with always!

recommendationsReceived.name: Bharathy Satish
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Database internals developer
recommendationsReceived.context: Bharathy worked with Dhamotharan on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: I worked with Dhamotharan for one year with Hewlett-Packard in sustenance team. During this tenure i see him as an excellant asset to the team. With his exceptional leadership qualities and dynamic decision making he helped me drive a customer escalation which had a critical fix. Also as a mentor he is a good contributor. [PS] Although he is a tough person to convince, but he has a strong point in his argument. I recommend him to every employee who wants to work with a friendly and skilled employer.

Everytime I contacted Dhamotharan for clarification on Backup issues, his explanations used to be very crisp, to the point and so clear which portrayed he exactly knew what he worked with.

recommendationsReceived.name: Thava Muni Alagu
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Enterprise Solutions Architect | AWS Professional | Gen AI Expert | Data Engineering | Data Driven Cloud Native Solutions
recommendationsReceived.context: Thava Muni was senior to Dhamotharan but didn't manage Dhamotharan directly

At Sun Microsystems, every time I assigned him challenging tasks, he was quick to take up the challenge and deliver great results on time. These are the tasks which involve dealing with complex multi-threaded systems which demands knowledge of strong data structures fundamentals and trouble shooting skills.  Well done, highly recommended engineer.

Its been great honor working under his guidance and I learnt a lot from him. Dhamo was always been a strong support for team. I am sure of his strengths and I also wish him a great luck.

summary: Dhamotharan Sritharan is currently a software engineering leader who shares a mix of technical deep-dives, community updates, and career news — he has recently announced a move to Senior Principal Software Engineer at Baffle, Inc. while also amplifying work tied to the Microsoft PostgreSQL OSS engine team and broader database ecosystems. His posts consistently focus on database internals (especially PostgreSQL), open source contribution, encryption and cybersecurity (including post-quantum concerns), and practical engineering roles and hiring. The tone alternates between technical explanation and thoughtful promotion of community contributors, with occasional personal reflections, career-seeking posts, and curated interviews or articles. He demonstrates domain expertise in C++/Rust-level systems work, database engine development, and data protection, while also surfacing industry conversations about blockchain and data warehousing. A recurring pattern is sharing concrete resources (blogs, interviews, published articles), thanking contributors, and promoting hiring or collaboration opportunities — signaling both technical credibility and community leadership. Overall, his content differentiates itself by blending engineering depth with community advocacy and timely industry commentary on databases, security, and developer careers.
highlights: Technical Leader / Senior Engineering (career transition to Baffle)
